title: Adding multiple tags from make.com
excerpt:
Hi all, 
I have a few tags I’d need to add to the respective column, but I struggle to understand how exactly the set up should be in make.com 
  
Was anyone able to add multiple tags? 
Thanks!

posts.author: alexblack (Alex Black)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,
I have a few tags I’d need to add to the respective column, but I struggle to understand how exactly the set up should be in make.com

image937×397 36.3 KB

Was anyone able to add multiple tags?

Thanks!

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
To add multiple tags to a specific column in Airtable using Make.com, we can set up a straightforward automation to handle this efficiently. Here’s a step-by-step approach to achieve this:

  1. Trigger Setup: Create a scenario that triggers when a new record is added or an existing record is updated in your Airtable base.
  2. Retrieve Tags: Use the “Search Records” module to fetch the tags you want to add.
  3. Update Record: Use the “Update Record” module to append the retrieved tags to the respective column in the target record.

Steps in Make.com:

  • Trigger: Airtable > Watch Records.
  • Search Module: Airtable > Search Records (to get the tags).
  • Update Module: Airtable > Update Record (append the tags to the column).

posts.author: alexblack (Alex Black)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you! Would you be so kind and show an example of the array of the “append the tags to the column” step?
